characters.physical_details: Latina; dark hair usually tied back; cracked sanitizer-dried hands; visibly tired.

key_relationships.lola_fernandez: resident whose performance she sees through
key_relationships.marcus_webb: outside distributor who can destroy her on paper
key_relationships.yolanda: ICU-nurse friend she stops confiding in

characters.occupation_status: Lakeview LPN; usually 3-11 with doubles; working toward RN; on day shift by chapter 18 while interviewing at a New Brunswick hospital.
characters.literacy_languages: English and Spanish; fluent in nursing and medication documentation.

state_transitions.change: Confronts Harry privately and gives the ring one week instead of reporting.
Lies to state surveyor Linda Horvath about the discrepancy pattern.
Hides Burt's hollowed dictionary stash before EMS arrives.
Destroys stock, notebooks, and phones to shut the ring down.
Moves to day shift and interviews outward, but is still at Lakeview.

characters.physical_details: White Polish-American; thin, angular, pale-eyed; faded blue cardigan, performance wheelchair, hip pain, cracking knees.

key_relationships.carmen_reyes: nurse who exposes and then protects him
key_relationships.agnes_chen: co-founder and pharmacology check on him
key_relationships.jack_zielinski: grandson whose landscaping debt he subsidizes
key_relationships.eleanor_zielinski: dead wife used in his daytime performance

characters.occupation_status: Retired accountant; Lakeview resident in single Room 114A; ring founder and organizer.
characters.literacy_languages: English; some Polish; keeps pencil records and finishes crosswords in ink.

state_transitions.change: Drops the dementia act for Carmen and negotiates directly.
Expels Marcus and explains the ring's medication-failure origin.
Agrees to destroy the ring's evidence, then is seen making a low, private call on a second hidden phone.
Still has sharp moments, but the sharpness no longer stays fully lit.

characters.physical_details: Chinese-American; small and thin; short gray hair, wire-rim glasses, slight left-side stroke drag; exact hands.

key_relationships.harry_zielinski: co-founder she trusts only when his arithmetic is sound
key_relationships.carmen_reyes: nurse she recruits into the information side
key_relationships.helen_chen: daughter who sold her house and institutionalized her
key_relationships.burt_kovacs: patient whose meds she refuses to divert once he loses capacity

characters.occupation_status: Retired pharmacist; Lakeview resident in 108A with a high-care roommate; ring safety and records authority until the cleanup.
characters.literacy_languages: English and Cantonese; reads charts and labels like a practicing pharmacist.

state_transitions.change: Drops the daytime nonverbal performance and tells Carmen she is fully competent.
Pulls Burt's prescriptions from active diversion and destroys the buffered stash.
Finds Marcus's wrong-number text and forces the confrontation.
Loses the four composition notebooks that were her only professional work since placement.
Stops eating normally and becomes markedly withdrawn after the ring ends.

characters.physical_details: Cuban-American; silver hair, warm face, gold crucifix; early Parkinson's with visible tremor.

key_relationships.tammy_briggs: daily confidante she feels guilty deceiving
key_relationships.tomas_fernandez: dead husband whose voice still structures her thinking
key_relationships.roberto_fernandez: son in Chicago who calls weekly
key_relationships.miriam_fernandez: daughter in Orlando who sends cards

characters.occupation_status: Retired fourth-grade teacher; Lakeview resident in 112A; ring lookout and cover story.

state_transitions.change: Realizes her performance is starting to blur with real word-finding loss.
Rejects Marcus's expansion and loses the safety of his cover-nephew role.
Stops performing warmth as easily; stays in her room and asks after Harry.

characters.physical_details: Black; tall but stooped; large hands; deep wet cough worsening across the fall.

key_relationships.burt_kovacs: roommate and the person he protects most directly
key_relationships.denise_washington: daughter in Trenton who depends on his monthly money order
key_relationships.maryann_washington: dead wife whose grave he visits monthly

characters.occupation_status: Retired steelworker and former union organizer; Lakeview resident in 116A; cash handler and cemetery handoff point until the ring ends.
characters.literacy_languages: English; keeps reading paperbacks, sometimes hidden inside old magazines.

state_transitions.change: Cough becomes a continuity-level health threat, but he still refuses to confirm it.
Finally submits to chest X-ray; results are still pending.

characters.physical_details: White Hungarian-American; wispy white hair, long musician's hands, real dementia, chronic pain, tremor, increasing frailty.

key_relationships.erzsebet_kovacs: dead wife whose dictionary becomes the stash
key_relationships.agnes_chen: the only ring member who draws a capacity line around his medications

characters.occupation_status: Retired jazz musician and trumpeter; Lakeview resident in 116A; his prescriptions are the ring's highest-value supply until his fall removes them from diversion.
characters.literacy_languages: English; Hungarian resurfaces as his dementia worsens.

state_transitions.change: Falls, gets six scalp sutures and a concussion, and returns markedly diminished.
Post-concussive decline becomes severe; he sleeps most of the day and rarely speaks English.
Remains unaware of the ring's end; the emptied dictionary is back on his shelf.

characters.physical_details: Black; neat and controlled; former-CNA body memory; basement apartment; worn older cars.

key_relationships.harry_zielinski: organizer who depends on him and eventually cuts him off
key_relationships.carmen_reyes: nurse whose threat to report limits him
key_relationships.agnes_chen: pharmacology veto he keeps trying to route around
key_relationships.derek: buyer managing diabetes and unstable work hours

characters.occupation_status: Former Lakeview CNA fired after reporting understaffing to the ombudsman; ring distributor through visitor-cover as Lola's nephew until expelled in chapter 16.
characters.literacy_languages: English; knows healthcare workflows from CNA work.

state_transitions.change: Secretly keeps pursuing a reseller after promising the group he will decline.
Wrong-number text exposes his separate high-volume pipeline; Harry cuts him off from the building.
Leaves Carmen a voicemail claiming he has found another way with no middlemen.

characters.availability: POV chapter 6; present or referenced through chapter 18.

characters.physical_details: White; sturdy; bleach-stained teal scrubs; hair usually in a knot; body worn by CNA work.

key_relationships.carmen_reyes: colleague she increasingly watches instead of trusting
key_relationships.lola_fernandez: resident she genuinely cares for and confides in
key_relationships.greg_briggs: ex-husband now seeing a much younger woman

characters.occupation_status: Lakeview CNA, 15 years, $16.50/hour; divorced and living alone in a cheap studio.

state_transitions.change: Starts recording Carmen's late-night patterns and the TV-room light.
Finds Carmen still at the building at dawn, smoke in her scrubs, and chooses silence.

characters.availability: Present from chapter 1 on; POV chapter 14.

characters.physical_details: Carmen's small, chatty son; sea-life obsessive with school projects about turtles, octopuses, and marine animals.
Desk-soft administrator in polos and khakis, usually appearing only when metrics, flowers, or survey optics matter.
characters.occupation_status: Lakeview administrator focused on documentation, survey scores, and census optics over care reality.
characters.availability: Visible in chapters 1 and 9; discussed elsewhere.

characters.physical_details: Indian-American, small and fast-moving; carries the pace of a physician covering too many patients across too many buildings.

key_relationships.carmen_reyes: relies on Carmen's assessments and charting

characters.occupation_status: Attending physician covering Lakeview and other facilities; biweekly rounds, overbooked schedule, ordering doctor of record for many residents.

state_transitions.change: Orders Agnes's nutritional workup and waits on Earl's chest X-ray results.

characters.availability: Visible in chapters 9 and 18; referenced elsewhere.

timeline.story_start: Late August 2026, during oppressive heat and failing AC at Lakeview Gardens.
timeline.estimated_span: Roughly late August through November 2026, with chapter 18 about three clean-count weeks after the chapter 17 cleanup.

seasonal_track.season: Late August to early September: oppressive heat, bad AC, summer still holding.
Mid-September to early October: school back in session, cooler nights, first leaves turning.
Late October to early November: cold nights, bare trees, parking lot dark early.
November day-shift light after three clean weeks and the ring's shutdown.

key_events.event: Agnes catches a dangerous ciprofloxacin-ropinirole interaction after the pharmacy switch; she and Harry start self-managing medications.
key_events.timing: Backstory, roughly 18 or more months before the main action

key_events.event: Marcus is fired after taking Lakeview understaffing concerns to the ombudsman.
Harry catches Carmen's tramadol error and reveals unexpected lucidity.
First documented count discrepancies and the midnight TV-room discovery.
Carmen sees Harry walking at 2:40 AM and confirms a larger diversion operation.
Carmen confronts Harry and agrees to one week instead of reporting immediately.
State survey; Carmen lies to Linda Horvath about the discrepancy pattern.
Burt falls, is hospitalized, and returns post-concussively diminished; his meds are removed from diversion.
Marcus secretly keeps a reseller-volume deal after promising he will decline it.
Wrong-number text exposes Marcus's separate high-volume pipeline; Harry expels him.
News of the reseller arrest triggers the overnight cleanup and formal end of the ring.
Three clean-count weeks later Carmen is on day shift, interviewing out, and receives Marcus's voicemail.

geography.primary_setting: Lakeview Gardens Rehabilitation and Nursing Center, off Route 27 in central New Jersey; no lake, no garden.

key_locations.name: Wing A
key_locations.details: Long-term-care hall with rooms 100-120A. Harry in 114A (single), Agnes in 108A, Lola in 112A, Earl and Burt in 116A. The sub-station cannot see the TV room around the bend.

key_locations.name: TV room
key_locations.details: Blind-spot common room at the far end of Wing A. Ring meetings happen here after midnight.

key_locations.name: Medication room and main station
key_locations.details: Small locked room with the controlled-substance cabinet, count trail, and the formal record under Carmen's license.

key_locations.name: Courtyard
key_locations.details: Paved rectangle with three scrubby trees, four benches, and a parking-lot view.

key_locations.name: Loading dock
key_locations.details: Rear concrete dock, out of sight from the front, used for late-night access and the chapter 17 burn/cleanup.

key_locations.name: Carmen's apartment
key_locations.details: Second-floor two-bedroom, about 25 minutes from Lakeview; tight kitchen and no financial slack.

key_locations.name: Route 1 strip-mall lot
key_locations.details: Marcus's regular handoff zone for buyers.

key_locations.name: Cemetery south of New Brunswick
key_locations.details: Maryann Washington's grave; monthly van outing and Earl-Marcus cemetery handoff point.

distances: Lakeview to Carmen's apartment: about 25 minutes.
Lakeview to Gloria's house: about 15 minutes.
Lakeview to Marcus's strip-mall handoff point: about 20 minutes south on Route 1.
Lakeview to Maryann's cemetery: about 40 minutes by facility van.
The TV room is around the bend from Wing A's sub-station and not visible from it.

world_rules: Controlled substances are counted every shift on paper logs and the hybrid electronic system; discrepancies land on the dispensing nurse's record.
LPN medication administration is documented under Carmen's license number, making her the first clean paper target if counts drift.
Residents can legally refuse medications; staff chart refusals and leave the order in place unless a doctor changes it.
State surveyors review charts, interview staff and residents, and usually stop at documentation findings unless hard evidence forces deeper investigation.
Visitor logs and loose visiting-hour enforcement make a relative-cover story workable if nobody cross-checks patterns.
Overnight staffing is thin enough that one nurse and one aide create blind spots the residents can exploit.
Fentanyl patches and mixed sedatives carry overdose risk; Agnes draws hard ethical lines around any sale likely to kill someone.
This is a realistic contemporary New Jersey nursing-home and healthcare world: no magic, only institutional failure, regulation, and uneven access.

power_structure.holder: New Jersey Board of Nursing / diversion enforcement

power_structure.mechanism: founder authority and control of timing

power_structure.over: what the ring can safely move
power_structure.mechanism: pharmacology, interaction screening, and record control
he is the only regular operator who can leave the building and meet buyers

power_structure.over: the ring's exposure after she discovers it
power_structure.mechanism: access to charts, counts, survey truth, and reporting channels

objects.origin: Gift from Erzsebet; Agnes hollows it into the stash.
objects.status: Active until Burt's fall; hidden in chapter 11; emptied and back on Burt's shelf by chapter 18.

objects.name: Cookie tin
objects.origin: Blue Danish butter cookie tin used as Marcus's visit cover and handoff container.
objects.status: Operational through the ring; dumped in chapter 17.

objects.name: Shared prepaid flip phone
objects.origin: Operational burner kept hidden in Harry's room.
objects.status: Wrong-number text exposes Marcus's side pipeline in chapter 16; destroyed in chapter 17.

objects.name: Agnes's four composition notebooks
objects.origin: Self-kept inventory, buyer-screening, and transaction records.
objects.status: Hidden under Agnes's mattress until Carmen burns them in chapter 17.

objects.name: Harry's second hidden phone
objects.origin: Separate device not shown to the ring during normal operations.
objects.status: Seen in Harry's hand after the cleanup and active again in chapter 18.

objects.name: Carmen's license badge
objects.status: She touches or adjusts it under stress; it tracks her professional exposure.

objects.name: Lola's gold crucifix
objects.origin: Family devotional object from her mother.
objects.status: Worn throughout; touched when she is anxious or watching a doorway.

objects.name: Earl's handkerchief
objects.origin: Plain cloth he uses to monitor the cough in private.
objects.status: Still checks clean through chapter 15; by chapter 17 he no longer looks before refolding it.

debts.status: Current; losing Carmen's license would collapse repayment.
Current; Carmen cannot miss it because the car is essential.
Harry sends about $500/month from ring money while the ring exists.

income_sources.reliability: Stable only while her license stays clean; doubles create the margin.

income_sources.amount: About $4,000 gross/month until chapter 17; Marcus took $1,200, Denise $400, Jack $500, Lola's clinic $350, Burt care $500, Agnes $400, and $650 stayed in reserve
income_sources.reliability: Operated for roughly 18 months; shut down in chapter 17.

income_sources.amount: $2,340/month gross, with only $50 left after Medicaid patient responsibility
income_sources.reliability: Stable but functionally unusable for family support.
Steady but not enough to feel secure.

knowledge_state.knows: The full ring membership, count manipulation, buyer structure, and that she lied to Linda Horvath.
Marcus left a chapter 18 voicemail saying he is back and has found another way with no middlemen.
Harry had a second hidden phone and was making a private dawn call after the chapter 17 cleanup.
She is cognitively intact, scored 30/30 on her MMSE, and was wrongly institutionalized after a recoverable stroke.
knowledge_state.learned_in: backstory; disclosed to Carmen in chapter 8

knowledge_state.knows: Something organized is happening around Carmen, Harry, Agnes, Lola, and the TV room after midnight; she has dates written down.
His cough may be serious enough to change his prescriptions and end the money flow to Denise, so he keeps minimizing it.
She is receiving $400 a month from what Earl calls a VA Aid and Attendance-style benefit.
hidden_from: the true source is the ring's drug money

knowledge_state.knows: Her mother had a stroke and needs institutional care.
hidden_from: Agnes recovered speech, scored 30/30, and is fully lucid

knowledge_state.knows: Her performed confusion is beginning to blur with real word loss.

environmental_constants: Lakeview's baseline smell is disinfectant over urine, warm skin, institutional food, and hand sanitizer.
Daylight is harsh fluorescent brightness; night is half-lit hallways and sodium-orange parking-lot spill.
Room 110 is a persistent sound source: low moaning or murmuring that staff eventually treat as building noise.
The courtyard is paved and bare, with three scrubby trees and a parking-lot view.
The building's heating and cooling are unreliable enough to shape behavior: summer heat lingers, fall cold leaks in, and everyone notices the systems failing.
